title: bothriocline misera
intro: Bothriocline misera, also known as the 'small-leaved hill balm', is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa, particularly in the regions of Cameroon and Nigeria. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Bothriocline misera can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to leverage its medicinal benefits.
